audit_metadata.stack: Laravel 12 + Inertia.js v2 + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4

strengths: Comprehensive admin panel with 25 controllers, audit logging, cache invalidation
Redis-locked billing mutations via BillingService prevent race conditions
DOMPurify used on all user-facing dangerouslySetInnerHTML (Blog, 2FA QR)
CSV injection protection in CsvExport class and AdminBillingController
No env() calls in app code — all config() lookups
is_admin/super_admin NOT in $fillable — mass assignment protection
Model::preventLazyLoading() enabled globally with production logging fallback
Security headers (CSP with nonce, HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY, Permissions-Policy)
Encrypted admin ID in impersonation session
Rate limiting on all auth and mutation endpoints
Feature flag system with route-dependent flag guards
GDPR personal data export with audit log
Webhook signature verification (HMAC-SHA256) with replay protection
top_risks: Retention coupon endpoint lacks Form Request validation
JSON-LD schemas use dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify (acceptable for dev-controlled content but fragile)
Notification API routes not gated by feature flag at route level
Webhook API routes not gated by feature flag at route level
Some admin operations missing super_admin on destructive actions
No RBAC beyond is_admin/super_admin (flat privilege model)

findings.title: Retention coupon endpoint missing Form Request validation
findings.description: applyRetentionCoupon() accepts a bare Request instead of a Form Request. While the coupon ID comes from config, the method is a mutation endpoint (POST) that directly calls Stripe's applyCoupon(). A malicious actor could potentially exploit this if the retention_coupon_id config is ever changed to accept request input.
findings.recommendation: Create ApplyRetentionCouponRequest with authorize() check and validation. Even if no request body params are needed, the Form Request provides consistent authorization and CSRF protection patterns.

findings.title: Notification API routes not gated by feature flag at route level
findings.description: Notification routes in api.php are always registered regardless of features.notifications.enabled. The comment says 'feature-gated in controller constructor' but this means the routes exist even when the feature is off, increasing attack surface.
findings.recommendation: Wrap notification routes in if (config('features.notifications.enabled')) block like other feature-gated routes.

findings.title: Webhook API routes not gated by feature flag at route level
findings.description: Webhook endpoint management routes (CRUD) are always registered regardless of features.webhooks.enabled. Exposes webhook management endpoints when feature is disabled.
findings.recommendation: Wrap webhook routes in if (config('features.webhooks.enabled')) block.

findings.title: Resume endpoint missing Form Request validation
findings.description: resume() accepts a bare Request instead of a dedicated Form Request with authorize() check. While auth middleware handles authentication, Form Requests provide consistent authorization validation.
findings.recommendation: Create ResumeSubscriptionRequest with authorize() method.

findings.title: Admin audit-logs/export route registered AFTER wildcard {auditLog}
findings.description: Route '/audit-logs/export' is defined AFTER '/audit-logs/{auditLog}' (line 139). In Laravel, the first matching route wins. The string 'export' would match the {auditLog} wildcard parameter first, causing a ModelNotFoundException or incorrect behavior. This is a route ordering bug.
findings.recommendation: Move the export route BEFORE the {auditLog} wildcard route, consistent with how other admin sections handle this (users, feedback, contact-submissions all place export before wildcard).

findings.title: BillingService::getSubscriptionStatus duplicates subscription query
findings.description: getSubscriptionStatus() calls resolveUserTier() which calls user->subscription('default') again, creating a redundant query. The method already has the subscription object but passes the user to resolveUserTier instead of reusing the loaded subscription.
findings.recommendation: Extract tier resolution from the already-loaded $subscription object to avoid the extra query.

findings.title: Trusted proxy configuration reads directly from $_SERVER/$_ENV
findings.description: The trusted proxy configuration reads TRUSTED_PROXIES directly from $_SERVER/$_ENV superglobals instead of using config(). While this works at boot time before config is loaded, it bypasses the config system's protection and could be manipulated by request headers in some server configurations.
findings.recommendation: Move trusted proxy configuration to a config file and use config() helper, or document the security assumption that these env vars cannot be influenced by request headers.

findings.title: Admin can create admin users without super_admin check in store()
findings.description: The store() method checks isSuperAdmin() in the Form Request's rules() to conditionally allow is_admin field. However, AdminCreateUserRequest::authorize() only checks isAdmin(). A regular admin could craft a request including is_admin=true — the rule won't be present so it gets filtered by validated(), but the intent should be explicit: only super_admin can set is_admin at all.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit super_admin check before setting is_admin in store(), or enforce via policy.

findings.title: Missing password confirmation on destructive billing operations
findings.description: Cancel, swap, and quantity update operations do not require password confirmation despite being high-impact financial mutations. An attacker with a session cookie could cancel a subscription.
findings.recommendation: Add password_confirmation or re-authentication middleware on cancel/swap/quantity endpoints.

findings.title: No rate limiting on admin billing portal redirect
findings.description: The portal() endpoint generates Stripe billing portal URLs without a specific rate limit. While the global throttle:60,1 from admin routes applies, the Stripe API call could be exploited for enumeration.
findings.recommendation: Add specific throttle middleware (e.g., throttle:5,1) on the billing portal route.

findings.title: toggleActive missing transaction wrapper
findings.description: toggleActive() performs delete/restore + audit log + cache invalidation but without a DB transaction. If the audit log dispatch fails, the state is inconsistent. bulkDeactivate/bulkRestore correctly use transactions.
findings.recommendation: Wrap toggleActive in DB::transaction() for consistency.

findings.title: ExportController.users() only exports current user's own data
findings.description: The users() export method filters ->where('id', $request->user()->id), meaning it only exports the current user's own record. The route name 'export.users' and endpoint '/export/users' suggests it should export multiple users. This is either a bug (should export team members) or confusing naming.
findings.recommendation: Either rename to 'export.my-data' or expand the query to export relevant users (e.g., team members if billing enabled).

findings.title: Admin user update allows updating trashed users inconsistently
findings.description: update() has abort_unless(! $user->trashed(), 403) but the route definition uses ->withTrashed(). This means the route resolves trashed users but then immediately aborts. While functionally correct, it's confusing — the route should not resolve trashed users if they can't be updated.
findings.recommendation: Remove ->withTrashed() from the update route, or move the check to middleware.

findings.title: Missing cache invalidation after retention coupon application
findings.description: applyRetentionCoupon() calls subscription->applyCoupon() but does not invalidate admin billing caches (BILLING_STATS, etc). This means admin dashboard shows stale subscription data.
findings.recommendation: Add $this->invalidateAdminCaches() after successful coupon application.

findings.title: Audit log uses string literal instead of AnalyticsEvent enum
findings.description: auditService->log('retention_coupon_applied', ...) uses a raw string instead of an AnalyticsEvent enum value. All other audit calls use the enum. This event won't be trackable by the analytics system.
findings.recommendation: Add RETENTION_COUPON_APPLIED to AnalyticsEvent enum and use it here.

findings.title: AdminCacheKey::flushAll() queries DB for every user with overrides
findings.description: flushAll() does a DB query to find all user IDs with feature flag overrides, then loops to forget each cache key. With many users, this is O(n) cache deletes.
findings.recommendation: Use cache tags or a prefix-based flush instead of per-user iteration.

findings.title: HandleInertiaRequests shares subscription data on every request
findings.description: When billing is enabled, the middleware loads subscriptions on every Inertia request to compute subscription status. This adds 1-2 queries per page load. While deferred via closures, Inertia still evaluates all shared props.
findings.recommendation: Cache subscription status per-user with short TTL (60s) to avoid per-request DB queries.

findings.title: EngagementScoringService called on every admin user index load
findings.description: scoreBatch() is called on every load of the admin users index page. If this service makes DB queries per user, it adds significant overhead to a frequently-accessed admin page.
findings.recommendation: Cache engagement scores or compute them asynchronously.

findings.title: Admin billing dashboard makes 6+ service calls per load
findings.description: The dashboard() method calls getDashboardStats(), getTierDistribution(), getStatusBreakdown(), getGrowthChart(), getTrialStats(), getCohortRetention(), plus a direct AuditLog query. Even with caching, cache misses cause 6+ complex queries.
findings.recommendation: Use Inertia deferred props for secondary data (charts, cohort retention) so the page loads fast with primary stats first.

findings.title: Two-factor routes always registered but feature-gated in controller
findings.description: Two-factor authentication settings routes are always registered regardless of features.two_factor.enabled. The comment says 'feature-gated in controller constructor'. This inconsistency with other feature-gated routes (billing, webhooks, api_tokens) creates confusion.
findings.recommendation: Either wrap in feature flag check for consistency, or document the rationale for controller-level gating.

findings.title: Webhook settings route always registered but feature-gated in controller
findings.description: Same issue as ARCH-001 — webhook settings page route registered unconditionally.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in if (config('features.webhooks.enabled')) for consistency.

findings.title: Subscription model extends Cashier but only adds one cast
findings.description: The custom Subscription model only adds a single past_due_since cast. This is fine but consider whether the model wrapper is justified for a single cast.
findings.recommendation: Keep for now — the custom model enables future extension and is registered via Cashier::useSubscriptionModel().

findings.title: FeatureFlagService has large method count
findings.description: FeatureFlagService handles resolution, validation, CRUD operations, and caching all in one class (14+ methods). Consider separating concerns.
findings.recommendation: Split into FeatureFlagResolver (read) and FeatureFlagManager (write) if the class grows further.

findings.title: No RBAC beyond admin/super_admin binary flags
findings.description: Authorization is limited to is_admin and super_admin boolean flags. No role-based access control or permission system exists. This is fine for a starter template but limits multi-team or complex permission scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Document this as a known limitation. Add RBAC (e.g., Spatie Permissions) when multi-role requirements emerge.

findings.title: UserPolicy only exists but is minimal
findings.description: UserPolicy exists but likely has minimal methods. Most authorization is done via middleware (admin/super_admin). This is inconsistent — some auth via policy, most via middleware.
findings.recommendation: Consider expanding UserPolicy for resource-level authorization on user management operations.

findings.title: JSON-LD schemas use dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify
findings.description: Multiple components (FaqJsonLd, BreadcrumbJsonLd, Guides pages) use dangerouslySetInnerHTML for JSON-LD without DOMPurify. While content is developer-controlled and DOMPurify corrupts JSON-LD, the </script> escape is the only protection.
findings.recommendation: Document the security rationale in a central location. The current approach (replace </script>) is correct for JSON-LD.

findings.title: Blog Show component allows 'class' attribute in DOMPurify allowlist
findings.description: DOMPurify ALLOWED_ATTR includes 'class' for blog content. While the comment explains this is admin-controlled Markdown, if user-generated content is ever displayed through this component, 'class' could be used for CSS-based attacks.
findings.recommendation: Add a runtime check or prop flag to disable 'class' when content source is user-generated.

findings.title: Missing error boundary wrapping on admin pages
findings.description: Admin pages with complex data visualization (charts, tables) should be wrapped in error boundaries to prevent full-page crashes from rendering errors in chart data.
findings.recommendation: Wrap chart/visualization components in ErrorBoundary with fallback UI.

findings.title: Large chart library potentially not code-split
findings.description: Chart components (AreaChart, BarChart, LineChart, PieChart) import charting libraries. If these are not lazy-loaded, they add to the initial bundle size for all pages.
findings.recommendation: Use React.lazy() for chart components, especially on admin pages that not all users see.

findings.title: 181 test files but coverage gaps likely in newer features
findings.description: The project has 181 test files which is excellent. However, newer features like NPS responses, contact submissions, email send logs, and lifecycle/engagement scoring may have incomplete edge case coverage.
findings.recommendation: Run php artisan test --coverage to identify untested code paths in newer features.

findings.title: No mutation testing in CI
findings.description: While infection (mutation testing) is mentioned in CLAUDE.md, it's not part of the CI pipeline. Mutation testing catches weak assertions that pass but don't actually verify behavior.
findings.recommendation: Add infection to CI pipeline, at least for critical paths (billing, auth).

findings.title: SQLite in tests vs MySQL in CI may mask database-specific issues
findings.description: Local tests use SQLite in-memory while CI uses MySQL 8.0. Some queries (especially those using DB::raw) may behave differently between SQLite and MySQL.
findings.recommendation: Consider running a subset of tests with MySQL locally, or ensure CI catches all database-specific issues.

findings.title: Session encryption only in production
findings.description: Session encryption is conditionally enabled only in production: env('SESSION_ENCRYPT', env('APP_ENV') === 'production'). This means staging/preview environments have unencrypted sessions.
findings.recommendation: Enable session encryption in all non-local environments to match production security posture.

findings.title: Well-structured admin cache invalidation system
findings.description: AdminCacheKey enum provides centralized cache key management with flushAll() and per-domain invalidation. CacheInvalidationManager wraps common patterns. This is a strong pattern.

findings.title: Impersonation security well-implemented
findings.description: Impersonation encrypts admin ID in session, prevents impersonating admins/super_admins/self/trashed users, regenerates session on start/stop, validates admin still exists on stop. Audit logged.

findings.title: GDPR compliance strong
findings.description: Personal data export includes all user data (settings, social accounts, tokens, webhooks, audit logs with truncation). purgePersonalData() scrubs PII from audit logs before force delete. Rate limited at 3/60min.

findings.title: Webhook signature verification includes replay protection
findings.description: Incoming webhook verification includes timestamp-based replay protection with configurable tolerance (default 300s). Stripe-specific timestamp extraction handles the t=...;v1=... format.

findings.title: Feature flag system prevents boot-time/runtime confusion
findings.description: ROUTE_DEPENDENT_FLAGS constant prevents DB overrides from enabling flags when routes aren't registered at boot time. This correctly handles the Laravel boot-time vs runtime config distinction.

findings.title: CSP with nonce-based script policy
findings.description: Content Security Policy uses Vite's CSP nonce for scripts, allows only self + analytics origins. frame-src and object-src set to 'none'. Configurable report-only mode and report-uri.

security.notes: Strong auth middleware, CSP, rate limiting, CSRF, session security. Minor gaps in Form Request coverage on billing endpoints.

architecture.notes: Clean service layer, feature flags, cache management. Minor inconsistencies in route-level vs controller-level feature gating.

data_integrity.notes: Transactions on bulk operations, cache invalidation patterns. Missing transaction on toggleActive, missing cache invalidation on retention coupon.

performance.notes: preventLazyLoading enabled, pagination centralized, eager loading on billing. Subscription data loaded per-request, engagement scoring not cached.

frontend.notes: DOMPurify on user content, TypeScript types, semantic tokens. Missing error boundaries on complex admin pages, potential bundle size from charts.

testing.notes: 181 test files, parallel execution, SQLite + MySQL coverage. No mutation testing in CI, potential gaps in newer features.

billing.notes: Redis-locked mutations, Stripe error handling, seat validation, concurrent operation protection. Missing password confirmation on destructive ops.

admin.notes: 25 controllers, audit logging on all actions, super_admin on destructive operations, CSV export with injection protection.
